creator: thindil@laeran.pl.eu.org
text:
(In reply to Neal Nelson from comment #2)

Hello and thank you for checking it.

> Things are like they are for a reason, which I think you have overlooked. Nimble is not "included" in the build.

Very possible that I overlooked something. That's why I ask for testing. ;) But the issue with Nimble is:

1. Install Nim 2.0 without nimble.
2. Try to build Nimble package with Nim 2.0

The problem is, that the Nimble repository doesn't contain all the code required to build Nimble. It must be downloaded during build process. Downloaded with... Nimble. :) There is that recursion about which I was writing about. Now, Nimble depends on an external package, outside the main Nim source code tree. Nimble is a part of Nim and I think it always should be included into the main package. But, that's only my opinion. ;)
It has its own repository, just for organization of the work, not as a separated project, like for example Conan and C. Is more like cargo and Rust for me.

About the report, do whatever you want. That's my proposition to upgrade Nim. And you are the boss here. ;) The upgrade works for me, I was testing it with some Nim packages, all work as expected. Just, I need others to do more tests.

> I believe that this is forbidden for ports as the sources are not under ports control and thus we just don't know what we're getting.

I don't know. I know only that several ports download something during build. Furthermore, I don't remember which one exactly, Wine was downloading only for additional options.
